feedback.testWarning: This is a test environment for preview purposes. Data can be deleted at any time.

call: Academia incentivizes publishing over peer review. That means too many submissions and not enough reviews, or reviews that are late and low quality.
1. **Editors set a cost** for submissions, priced in review tokens.
2. **Authors earn tokens** by volunteering for venues, sharing expertise and availability, and submitting on-time, quality reviews, on whatever reviewing platform a venue uses.

3. **Authors spend tokens** to submit manuscripts for review, splitting the cost with co-authors. Or, they can gift tokens to individuals or shared pools to strategically support newcomers, colleagues in crisis, or emerging topics.

Because authors need to submit high-quality, on-time reviews to submit, authors get better, faster reviews, editors do less nagging, communities overall have a fairer distribution of peer review labor.

tip.browse: **Interested in adopting?** See the [journals and conferences](/venues) using the platform and reach out to their editors for their experiences. Or, [log in](/login) to propose a journal or conference adopt *Reciprocal Reviews*.
tip.about: **We are a volunteer community** of academics, not a for-profit organization. Our only goal is to improve the sustainability, quality, and integrity of peer review. See our [about](/about) page if you're interested in helping.
tip.track: **Don't want to set a cost?** Use Reciprocal Reviews to track volunteer reviewers and expertise in your community, streamlining reviewing and program committee invitations.

feedback.beta: We're in development! A public beta is likely in 2026. Visit [GitHub](https://github.com/reciprocalreviews) to see who's involved, see our [beta milestone](https://github.com/reciprocalreviews/reciprocalapp/issues?q=is:open%20milestone:%22Beta%22) for the work that remains. Subscribe to our low-volume [newsletter](https://reciprocalreviews.substack.com/) to follow along, check the [updates](/updates) page to see our weekly progress. And if you feel like helping, email us! We're a small volunteer group of academics.

venues.description: These are journals and conferences using Reciprocal Reviews to track and incentivize peer review. Choose one to see it's policies or volunteer to review for it, or propose a new one.
